title: 5.6 The root Test
notes: You can make a comparison with the geometric series again, by how much is the n-term being similar to the $r^n$ for some r. Therefore, you can see that $r=\sqrt[n]{a_n}$

steps.label: Theorem 5.17
steps.subtitle: Root Test
steps.bodyHtml:
Consider the series \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}a_n\). Let \[\rho=\lim_{n\to\infty}\sqrt[n]{|a_n|}\]
  1. If \(0\leq\rho < 1\), then \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}a_n\) converges
  2. If \(\rho > 1\) or \(\rho=\infty\), then \(\displaystyle\sum_{n=1}^{\infty}a_n\) diverges
  3. If \(\rho=1\), the test does not provide any information

steps.label: Warning
steps.subtitle: application issues
steps.bodyHtml:
1. The main issue is to use the root test on factorial. Never do that.
  2. The main application is when your n-term is the n-th power of an element, think of it like \(a_n=(f(n))^n\)
